title: <div>   * Quantity Needed: (Number) - Consider adding this next to each item in
content:
* Quantity Needed: (Number) - Consider adding this next to each item in the "Items Needed" field, or allow for multiple item entries.
* Preferred Pick-Up Location/Delivery Preference: (Short answer text/Dropdown: "Warehouse Pick-up," "Direct to Job Site," "Need Delivery") - Helps with logistics.
* Any Special Instructions/Notes: (Paragraph text)
* Instructions for Form Users: Clearly state at the top of the form: "Please fill out this form for all equipment and supply requests for the following day's work. For urgent needs today, please fill out this form AND contact [Your Name/Dad's Name] directly via phone after submitting."
B. Setting Up the Google Sheet (Master Request Log):
* When you create a Google Form, it automatically links to a Google Sheet where all responses are collected. This will be your "Master Request Log."
* Columns: The columns will automatically populate based on your form fields.
* Access: Share this Google Sheet with yourself, your father, and anyone responsible for purchasing/dispatching. Set viewing permissions appropriately (e.g., employees can only fill out the form, not edit the sheet directly).
context: From document: OK, I have been taken in to my father‘s company, Majestic design. It’s… — chunk 17 (chunk 17/22)
